title: How to use slirp4netns with podman-py?
body:
`slirp4netns` is used in `podman run`  like this:

```bash
python -m http.server 8080
```
```bash
podman run --network slirp4netns:allow_host_loopback=true --cap-add=IPC_LOCK --cap-add=NET_RAW docker.io/curlimages/curl curl host.containers.internal:8080
```

How can I achieve the same in podman-py?

comments.body:
Solution is using `network_options`.

```python
from podman import PodmanClient

podman_client = PodmanClient(base_url='unix:///run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock')

instance = podman_client.containers.create(
    "docker.io/curlimages/curl",
    ["curl","host.containers.internal:8080"],
    name="test",
    hostname="Test",
    cap_add=["IPC_LOCK", "NET_RAW"],
    networks={"slirp4netns": {}},
    network_options={"slirp4netns": ["allow_host_loopback=true"]},
    auto_remove=True,
)

instance.start()

logs = instance.logs(stdout=True, stderr=True, stream=True)
for line in logs:
    print(line)
```
